panels.description: Rate of incoming query (read) requests per second. Spike without latency increase is normal scaling; combined spike indicates saturation.
panels.title: Query Request Rate

panels.description: Search request latency percentiles for /select handlers. Alert fires at p99 > 1000ms (SolrHighSearchLatency).
panels.title: Search Latency p50 / p95 / p99

panels.description: Rate of incoming indexing (write/update) requests per second.
panels.title: Indexing Rate

panels.description: p99 latency for /update (indexing) requests. High latency may indicate merge pressure or I/O bottlenecks.
panels.title: Update Latency p99

panels.description: Number of permanently loaded Solr cores across selected instances.
panels.title: Active Cores

panels.description: Percentage of disk space free (min across instances). Alert fires below 15% for 5 minutes (SolrLowDiskSpace).
panels.title: Disk Free

panels.description: JVM heap memory in use per instance and pool. Uses max() to deduplicate dual OTel JVM scopes (java8/java17) emitted by Solr.
panels.title: Heap Used

panels.description: JVM heap memory committed (reserved from OS) per instance. Uses max() to avoid double-counting.
panels.title: Heap Committed

panels.description: Maximum JVM heap size (-Xmx) per instance.
panels.title: Heap Max

panels.description: p99 GC pause duration. Alert fires when total GC time > 10s/min for 3 minutes (SolrJvmGcThrashing).
panels.title: GC Pause p99

panels.description: GC collection frequency per collector. Frequent major GC indicates memory pressure.
panels.title: GC Collection Rate

panels.description: JVM thread count by state per instance. Large BLOCKED counts indicate lock contention.
panels.title: JVM Threads

panels.description: Recent JVM CPU utilization (0-1) per instance. Sustained values near 1.0 indicate CPU saturation.
panels.title: JVM CPU Utilization

panels.description: Pending ops in Overseer collection queue. Alert fires >50 for 5 min (SolrOverseerQueueBuildup). Only emitted in SolrCloud mode.
panels.title: Overseer Collection Work Queue

panels.description: Pending cluster state updates in Overseer queue. High values indicate ZooKeeper write pressure.
panels.title: Overseer State Update Queue

panels.description: Number of shard replicas where this instance is the current leader.
panels.title: Shard Leaders on Node

panels.description: Rate of ZooKeeper operations per instance. High rates may indicate excessive ZK polling.
panels.title: ZooKeeper Ops Rate

panels.description: Transaction logs remaining to replay during leader recovery. Non-zero indicates a replica catching up.
panels.title: Update Log Replay Remaining

panels.description: Lucene segment count per core. High counts (>50) degrade search performance.
panels.title: Segment Count per Core

panels.description: Total Lucene index size in MB across all selected cores.
panels.title: Total Index Size

panels.description: Index as % of available MMap memory (RAM minus heap). Above 70% (yellow) risks page-cache pressure; above 85% (red) causes I/O bottlenecks. Requires jvm_system_memory_total_bytes (Solr 10.x physical RAM metric). Shows No data if absent.
panels.title: MMap Efficiency

panels.description: Rate of IndexWriter segment flushes/second. High rates indicate heavy write load.
panels.title: Flush Rate

panels.description: Rate of completed minor (small segment) Lucene merge operations/second.
panels.title: Minor Merge Rate

panels.description: Rate of completed major (large segment) Lucene merges/second. Sustained major merges impact query latency.
panels.title: Major Merge Rate

panels.description: Searchable documents per core. Tracks index growth over time.
panels.title: Documents Indexed

panels.description: Documents added but not yet committed and visible to searchers. High values indicate delayed hard commits.
panels.title: Pending Commit Docs

panels.description: Filter cache hit rate. Below 80% suggests cache too small or too varied queries.
panels.title: Filter Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Query result cache hit rate. High hit rates reduce CPU for repeated queries.
panels.title: Query Result Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Document cache hit rate. Relevant when stored fields are frequently fetched.
panels.title: Document Cache Hit Rate

panels.description: Filter cache eviction rate. Sustained evictions indicate the cache is too small.
panels.title: Filter Cache Evictions

panels.description: Query result cache eviction rate. High rates with low hit rates indicate insufficient cache size.
panels.title: Query Result Cache Evictions

panels.description: Document cache eviction rate per second.
panels.title: Document Cache Evictions

panels.description: Total RAM consumed by each Solr cache type (filterCache, queryResultCache, documentCache).
panels.title: Cache RAM Used
